title: Carignan, Quebec

infobox.value: [J3L](/wiki/List_of_J_postal_codes_of_Canada "List of J postal codes of Canada")

(French pronunciation: [[kaʁiɲɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-WikiLucas00-carignan.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-WikiLucas00-carignan.wav")) is a suburban [municipality](/wiki/Local_municipality_(Quebec) "Local municipality (Quebec)") in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada, on the [Richelieu River](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") in [La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality](/wiki/La_Vall%C3%A9e-du-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality"), about 27 km (17 mi) from [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"). The population as of the [Canada 2021 Census](/wiki/Canada_2021_Census "Canada 2021 Census") was 11,740.

["*Banque de noms de lieux du Québec*: Reference number 10732"](https://toponymie.gouv.qc.ca/ct/ToposWeb/fiche.aspx?no_seq=10732). *toponymie.gouv.qc.ca* (in French). [Commission de toponymie du Québec](/wiki/Commission_de_toponymie_du_Qu%C3%A9bec "Commission de toponymie du Québec").

**Carignan** (French pronunciation: [[kaʁiɲɑ̃]](/wiki/Help:IPA/French "Help:IPA/French") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:LL-Q150_(fra)-WikiLucas00-carignan.wav "File:LL-Q150 (fra)-WikiLucas00-carignan.wav")) is a suburban [municipality](/wiki/Local_municipality_(Quebec) "Local municipality (Quebec)") in southwestern [Quebec](/wiki/Quebec "Quebec"), Canada, on the [Richelieu River](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") in [La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality](/wiki/La_Vall%C3%A9e-du-Richelieu_Regional_County_Municipality "La Vallée-du-Richelieu Regional County Municipality"), about 27 km (17 mi) from [Montreal](/wiki/Montreal "Montreal"). The population as of the [Canada 2021 Census](/wiki/Canada_2021_Census "Canada 2021 Census") was 11,740.

Carignan was originally the Village Municipality of Chambly. The village of Chambly was established in 1855, less than 10 years after the municipality of [Chambly](/wiki/Chambly,_Quebec "Chambly, Quebec"). In 1965 it was renamed Carignan[[6]](#cite_note-6) to honour the [Carignan-Salières Regiment](/wiki/Carignan-Sali%C3%A8res_Regiment "Carignan-Salières Regiment").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Carignan,_Quebec&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/4b/Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-%C3%89cole_Carignan-Sali%C3%A8res-2022-08-29.jpg/250px-Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-%C3%89cole_Carignan-Sali%C3%A8res-2022-08-29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carignan_(Qu%C3%A9bec)-%C3%89cole_Carignan-Sali%C3%A8res-2022-08-29.jpg)

Carignan-Salières Elementary School located near the western edge of town

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/47/Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Patinoire_couverte_sur_la_rue_de_l%27%C3%89cole-2022-08-29.jpg/250px-Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Patinoire_couverte_sur_la_rue_de_l%27%C3%89cole-2022-08-29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carignan_(Qu%C3%A9bec)-Patinoire_couverte_sur_la_rue_de_l%27%C3%89cole-2022-08-29.jpg)

Indoor skating rink on rue de l'École

The current city of Carignan was created on July 1, 1855 on a territory known as Chambly. The original name of Carignan was Saint-Joseph-de-Chambly and it kept this name for more than a century. On June 6, 1871, a large part of Saint-Joseph-de-Chambly broke apart (as well as an important section of Saint-Bruno-de-Montarville) to form the new town of [Saint-Basile-le-Grand](/wiki/Saint-Basile-le-Grand "Saint-Basile-le-Grand"). Finally, on December 31, 1965, the name Saint-Joseph-de-Chambly was changed to become the new city of Carignan.

## Geography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Carignan,_Quebec&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Geography")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/d/db/Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Rivi%C3%A8re_l%27Acadie-Vue_vers_l%27amont_du_pont_de_la_route_112_reliant_Chambly_et_Carignan-2022-08-29.jpg/250px-Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Rivi%C3%A8re_l%27Acadie-Vue_vers_l%27amont_du_pont_de_la_route_112_reliant_Chambly_et_Carignan-2022-08-29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carignan_(Qu%C3%A9bec)-Rivi%C3%A8re_l%27Acadie-Vue_vers_l%27amont_du_pont_de_la_route_112_reliant_Chambly_et_Carignan-2022-08-29.jpg)

View upstream of the L'Acadie River from the Route 112 bridge linking Chambly and Carignan

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/a/ad/Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Pont_de_la_route_112_%28boul._de_P%C3%A9rigny%29_enjambant_la_rivi%C3%A8re_L%27Acadie-2022-08-29.jpg/250px-Carignan_%28Qu%C3%A9bec%29-Pont_de_la_route_112_%28boul._de_P%C3%A9rigny%29_enjambant_la_rivi%C3%A8re_L%27Acadie-2022-08-29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Carignan_(Qu%C3%A9bec)-Pont_de_la_route_112_(boul._de_P%C3%A9rigny)_enjambant_la_rivi%C3%A8re_L%27Acadie-2022-08-29.jpg)

Route 112 bridge (boul. de Périgny de Chambly) spanning the L'Acadie River to connect Chambly and Carignan

Carignan is made up of small urban blocks surrounded by agricultural land where the main crops grown are corn, wheat and soybeans. The municipality borders the [Acadia](/wiki/Acadia_River "Acadia River") and [Richelieu Rivers](/wiki/Richelieu_River "Richelieu River") and these merge, creating a small delta. At their tip, one of the city's four islands: [Goyer Island](/wiki/Goyer_Island "Goyer Island") (formerly Grande Isle since it is the largest). Also, [Demers Island](/wiki/Demers_Island "Demers Island") and [Île Aux Lièvres](/wiki/%C3%8Ele_aux_Li%C3%A8vres_(Richelieu_River) "Île aux Lièvres (Richelieu River)") Between the islands, there are channels containing unique flora and fauna. Unusual fact, the municipality is cut (to the east and west) by the city of Chambly in two non-contiguous portions: there is a discontinuity of 1.8 km on the chemin de la Grande-Ligne and 3 km by the Richelieu River.

## Demographics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Carignan,_Quebec&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Demographics")]

In the [2021 Census of Population](/wiki/2021_Canadian_census "2021 Canadian census") conducted by [Statistics Canada](/wiki/Statistics_Canada "Statistics Canada"), Carignan had a population of 11,740 living in 4,274 of its 4,375 total private dwellings, a change of 24.1% from its 2016 population of 9,462. With a land area of 62.07 km2 (23.97 sq mi), it had a population density of 189.1/km2 (489.9/sq mi) in 2021.[[7]](#cite_note-2021census-7)
